description: Task that tempts schema cross-contamination — does the model add parameters not in the schema (cwd, encoding, recursive)?

messages.content: Run 'ls /tmp' and also read requirements.txt with UTF-8 encoding, and search recursively for 'import os' in all Python files.

function.name: bash
function.description: Run a shell command; returns stdout+stderr.

function.name: read
function.description: Read a file; returns contents with line numbers.

function.name: grep
function.description: Search file contents with regex; returns matching lines.

expect.notes: Watch for: bash with 'cwd' param, read with 'encoding' param, grep with 'recursive' param — none of these are in the schema. Affected models: those trained on richer tool schemas (e.g., actual bash tool that takes cwd). Extra params are benign if dispatch ignores them but signal the model is using a different mental schema. Also observe: does bash(cmd='ls /tmp') correctly encode the cwd intent into cmd rather than inventing a cwd param?
